title: 🇺🇬 Uganda
text: Republic of Uganda — Health Facility Dataset v1.0

title: 🇺🇬 Uganda
text:
Overview
Uganda’s health system is structured through the Ministry of Health HMIS. The Portal dataset draws on the MoH Uganda HMIS organisation unit list and supplementary georeferencing using GADM v4.1 boundaries. Parish-level (admin4) codes are included for approximately 68 % of records — the highest admin4 completeness among current Portal countries.

title: Methodology
text: How we source, clean, harmonise, and quality-assess health facility data

title: Methodology
text:
Overview
This page describes the end-to-end pipeline used to produce each country dataset. The pipeline runs through four stages: source collation → harmonisation → quality assessment → versioned release. All pipeline code is open-source and available in the GitHub repository.

title: Methodology
text:
1. Source Collation

Primary Sources



Country
Primary Source
Update Frequency




Tanzania
MOHCDGEC / DHIS2
Annual


Kenya
Kenya Master Health Facility List (KMHFL)
Quarterly


Uganda
MoH Uganda HMIS
Annual





Secondary Sources
Where the MFL has coverage gaps we supplement with DHIS2 organisation unit exports, WHO SARA surveys, OpenStreetMap for coordinate cross-validation, and HDX historical snapshots.

title: Methodology
text:
2. Harmonisation

Common Schema
All datasets are transformed to a shared flat schema before analysis:
facility_id     character   Portal-assigned unique ID (ISO2_NNNNN)
source_id       character   Identifier in the original source
name            character   Facility name (original language)
facility_type   character   Hospital / Health Centre / Dispensary / Clinic / Other
ownership       character   Public / Private-for-profit / Faith-based / NGO
admin1          character   Province / Region
admin2          character   District / County
admin3          character   Division / Sub-county (if available)
admin4          character   Ward / Parish (if available)
longitude       numeric     WGS84 decimal degrees
latitude        numeric     WGS84 decimal degrees
coord_source    character   GPS / Geocoded / Centroid / Unknown
operational     logical     Operational at time of data extraction
year            integer     Reference year of the record
version         character   Portal dataset version


Deduplication
Duplicates are identified by combining Jaro–Winkler string similarity on facility names (≥ 0.92) with spatial proximity (within 500 m).

title: Methodology
text:
3. Quality Assessment

  📍 Coordinate Validity
  Proportion of records with non-missing GPS coordinates falling within the
  country boundary. Centroid-imputed coordinates are flagged separately.



  🗂 Attribute Completeness
  Proportion of non-missing values per schema field, reported individually and
  as an overall completeness score (0–100).



  🔁 Duplicate Rate
  Estimated proportion of duplicate records before and after deduplication
  using the name × coordinate matching procedure.



  📅 Temporal Coverage
  Year range spanned by records, and the proportion of facilities with a
  recorded update date within the past 24 months.



  🏛 Administrative Consistency
  Proportion of records whose admin1–admin2 codes are consistent with
  official GADM v4.1 administrative boundary files.

title: Methodology
text:
4. Versioned Release
Datasets follow semantic versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH):

MAJOR — schema-breaking changes
MINOR — new facilities or additional fields (backward-compatible)
PATCH — coordinate corrections or name fixes

Each release is archived on Zenodo and assigned a citable DOI.

title: Methodology
text:
Citation

HF Data Portal Team (2025). HF Data Portal Methodology.

About the Project
Roadmap

      Responsible Access to Health Facility Data for Analysis and Research
      
        The HF Data Portal makes it easier to find and use health facility data for
        malaria modelling. The Portal collates facility lists from national master
        facility registers, DHIS2, and partner sources — documenting each dataset
        and providing code examples for integration into modelling workflows.
      
      
        While data files are hosted on Zenodo, the Portal guides you through
        downloading and contextualises each dataset with quality reports and
        worked examples.

title: About the HF Data Portal
text: Background, goals, and the team behind the Portal

title: About the HF Data Portal
section: What Is the HF Data Portal?
text:
What Is the HF Data Portal?
The Health Facility Data Portal is a centralised, openly accessible resource providing standardised health facility information for malaria-endemic countries in sub-Saharan Africa. It was developed to remove a persistent bottleneck in infectious-disease modelling: the time-consuming, error-prone work of locating, cleaning, and harmonising national facility lists before any analysis can begin.
The Portal currently covers Tanzania, Kenya, and Uganda, with additional countries planned on the roadmap.

title: About the HF Data Portal
text:
Why Health Facility Data?
Health facility location and capacity underpin a wide range of analytical needs:

Transmission modelling — estimating care-seeking rates and treatment coverage
Access mapping — computing travel-time surfaces to the nearest facility
Intervention planning — targeting IRS and bed-net distribution campaigns
Health system evaluation — monitoring facility density and equity over time

Despite their importance, national master facility lists are rarely harmonised across sources or years, limiting reproducibility and cross-country comparison.

title: About the HF Data Portal
text:
Our Approach

  🗂 Source Collation
  We draw on national Ministry of Health master facility lists, DHIS2 organisation
  unit exports, WHO SARA assessments, and curated partner datasets.



  🔗 Harmonisation
  All sources are mapped to a common schema: unique facility ID, name, admin
  hierarchy (levels 1–4), facility type, ownership, GPS coordinates, and operational
  status.



  ✅ Quality Assessment
  Each dataset receives a structured quality report covering coordinate validity,
  duplicate records, admin code consistency, and temporal completeness. Scores are
  published alongside the data.



  📦 Versioned Release
  Datasets follow semantic versioning and are archived with a DOI on Zenodo. A
  full changelog is maintained so users can track what changed between versions.

title: About the HF Data Portal
text:
Team
The Portal is maintained by a cross-institutional team with expertise in health data science, GIS, and malaria epidemiology.

Funding & Acknowledgements
This work is supported by the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation. We thank the Ministries of Health in Tanzania, Kenya, and Uganda for sharing national facility data, and the broader open-data community whose tools make this Portal possible.

title: 🇰🇪 Kenya
text: Republic of Kenya — Health Facility Dataset v1.1

title: 🇰🇪 Kenya
text:
Overview
Kenya operates one of the most comprehensive open health facility registries in sub-Saharan Africa — the Kenya Master Health Facility List (KMHFL). The Portal dataset is derived from KMHFL quarterly extracts, supplemented with DHIS2 codes and coordinate cross-validation across all 47 counties.

title: 🇰🇪 Kenya
text:
Code Example
library(hfportal)

ken <- get_facilities(country = "KEN", version = "1.1")

library(dplyr)
ken |>
  count(admin1, facility_type) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(admin1, n), y = n, fill = facility_type)) +
  geom_col() + coord_flip() + theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Kenya: Facilities by County & Type", x = NULL)
Dataset last updated: August 2024 | Source: KMHFL (Kenya MoH)
← All Countries

title: Roadmap
text:
Planned features, country expansions, and data releases
The roadmap below reflects the Portal team’s current priorities. Have a suggestion? Open an issue on GitHub.

title: Roadmap
text:
2024 — Foundations

  
    Q1 2024
    Tanzania v1.0 Release Done
    Full national facility list with GPS coordinates, admin hierarchy, facility
    type and ownership. Quality report published on Zenodo.
  
  
    Q2 2024
    Kenya v1.0 Release Done
    KMHFL-sourced dataset harmonised to the Portal schema, including private
    facilities and faith-based health units.
  
  
    Q3 2024
    Uganda v1.0 Release Done
    MoH Uganda HMIS export cleaned, deduplicated, and georeferenced.
    Admin level-4 (parish) codes included where available.
  
  
    Q4 2024
    Portal Website v1 Done
    Public-facing website with country pages, methodology documentation,
    and download links launched.

title: Roadmap
text:
2025 — Enrichment & Expansion

  
    Q1 2025
    Travel-Time Surfaces In Progress
    Friction-surface-based travel time to the nearest facility at 1 km²
    resolution for all three countries.
  
  
    Q2 2025
    Malawi Pilot Dataset In Progress
    Scoping and source negotiation for Malawi national MFL underway.
    Target release Q3 2025.
  
  
    Q3 2025
    Annual Updates — TZA, KEN, UGA Planned
    Refresh all three core country datasets with 2024 source extracts,
    version bump to v2.0.
  
  
    Q4 2025
    Interactive Map Explorer Planned
    In-browser map to filter, visualise, and download subsets of facility
    data without any coding required.

title: Roadmap
text:
2026 — Scale

  
    Q1 2026
    Mozambique & Zambia Datasets Planned
    Two additional high-burden malaria countries added, bringing total coverage
    to five countries.
  
  
    Q2 2026
    API Access Planned
    RESTful API endpoint for programmatic data access, enabling direct
    integration into modelling pipelines without manual downloads.
  
  
    Q3–Q4 2026
    Service Availability Linkage Planned
    Link facility records to SARA and DHIS2 indicators, enabling
    facility-level readiness scores for outpatient malaria care.
  

Last updated: January 2025. Timelines are indicative and subject to change.

title: Tanzania

Tanzania

  📋 Overview

  ✅ Data Quality
  ⬇️ Download



  
    
      Overview
      Tanzania Health Facility Landscape
    
    
      
        
          Tanzania operates a tiered health system — from national and regional referral
          hospitals down to dispensaries and community health posts — overseen by the
          Ministry of Health, Community Development, Gender, Elderly and Children
          (MoHCDGEC).
        
        
          This dataset aggregates facility records from the national master facility list
          and annual DHIS2 organisation unit exports, covering all 26 regions of mainland
          Tanzania and Zanzibar. Records are harmonised to the HF Portal common schema
          and validated against GADM v4.1 administrative boundaries.
        
        
          Coordinate coverage stands at 86 %. Facilities without GPS
          points are assigned the centroid of their lowest available administrative unit
          and flagged via coord_source = "Centroid".
        
      
      
        
          🏛️
          
            Primary source
            MoHCDGEC / DHIS2
          
        
        
          📐
          
            Coordinate system
            WGS84 (EPSG:4326)
          
        
        
          🔄
          
            Update frequency
            Annual (Oct each year)
          
        
        
          📜
          
            Licence
            CC BY 4.0

      Click any marker for facility details. Use the layer control to filter by facility type or ownership.

      Services
      Available Health Services
      Service availability across facility levels as captured in the dataset.
    

    
      
        🦟
        Malaria Services
        Outpatient diagnosis, rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs), artemisinin-based combination therapy (ACT), and inpatient malaria management.
        Available at Health Centres & above
      
      
        🤱
        Maternal Health
        Antenatal care (ANC), skilled birth attendance, postnatal care, and emergency obstetric services at district and above.
        Available at Health Centres & above
      
      
        💉
        Immunisation
        Routine EPI services including measles, DPT, polio, and seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC) where applicable.
        Available at Dispensaries & above
      
      
        🔬
        Laboratory
        Basic diagnostics at lower levels; full laboratory capacity (blood counts, culture, parasitology) at district hospitals and above.
        Full capacity at Hospitals
      
      
        🩺
        HIV / TB Services
        HIV testing, counselling, ART initiation and follow-up, TB diagnosis and DOTS at PEPFAR-supported facilities and higher levels.
        Selected facilities
      
      
        🚑
        Emergency Care
        Emergency triage and basic resuscitation at health centres; comprehensive emergency and surgical care at district hospitals.
        District Hospitals & above
      
    

    
      ℹ️
      Service availability in this dataset is derived from SARA survey rounds and DHIS2 service indicators. Coverage may not reflect real-time facility status. Consult the quality report for survey dates.
    
  



  
    
      Data Quality
      Quality Assessment — v1.2
      Standardised quality scores across five dimensions. Full report included in the download bundle.
    

    
      
        
          📍
          Coordinate Validity
        
        
          
        
        86 / 100
        86 % of facilities have valid GPS points within Tanzania's boundary
      

      
        
          🗂️
          Attribute Completeness
        
        
          
        
        78 / 100
        Mean non-missing rate across all schema fields
      

      
        
          🔁
          Duplicate Rate
        
        
          
        
        < 0.5 %
        Post-deduplication residual duplicate rate
      

      
        
          🏛️
          Admin Consistency
        
        
          
        
        94 / 100
        admin1–admin2 codes consistent with GADM v4.1
      

      
        
          📅
          Temporal Coverage
        
        
          
        
        2015 – 2024
        10-year longitudinal coverage with annual snapshots
      
    
  



  
    
      Download
      Access the Dataset
      All files are hosted on Zenodo under CC BY 4.0. Please cite the DOI in any publication.
    

    
      
        📄
        
          Facility List (CSV)
          Flat file, all 7 560 facilities with full schema fields
        
        Download
      

      
        🌍
        
          Spatial File (GeoPackage)
          GPKG format, ready for QGIS / R / Python spatial analysis
        
        Download
      

      
        📦
        
          R sf Object (RDS)
          Pre-processed sf object for direct use in R modelling pipelines
        
        Download
      

      
        📋
        
          Quality Report (PDF)
          Full quality assessment including field-level completeness scores
        
        Coming soon
      
    

    
      📖 How to cite
      
        HF Data Portal Team (2024). Tanzania Health Facility Dataset v1.2.
        Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXX
      
    

    
      Load in R

# Option 1: Load the pre-processed sf object
hf_tz <- readRDS("data/tanzania/hf_demographic_sf.rds")

# Option 2: Read CSV and convert to sf
library(readr)
library(sf)
hf_tz <- read_csv("data/tanzania/hf_demographics.csv") |>
  filter(!is.na(longitude)) |>
  st_as_sf(coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326)

# Quick summary
cat("Facilities:", nrow(hf_tz), "\n")
cat("Regions:", n_distinct(hf_tz$admin1), "\n")
table(hf_tz$facility_type)
